title: GitHub API の 500 エラー：原因と解決策
reasons: The article accurately describes the general definition of a 500 Internal Server Error as a server-side issue.

It correctly identifies GitHub's rate limits (5000 requests per hour for authenticated users) and the presence of rate limit headers (`X-RateLimit-Limit`, `X-RateLimit-Remaining`, `X-RateLimit-Reset`) in API responses.
The explanation of GraphQL API error handling (200 OK status with an 'errors' field in the body) is correct.
However, the article falsely claims that exceeding GitHub API rate limits can result in a 500 error; official documentation states 403 Forbidden or 429 Too Many Requests.

Claims that invalid JSON payloads, improper API version specification/missing headers, or insufficient Personal Access Token (PAT) scopes can lead to a 500 error are not consistently supported by official GitHub documentation, which typically indicates 400 Bad Request, 410 Gone, or 403 Forbidden for these client-side issues.
one or more claims are unsupported or unverifiable
required_actions: Correct the information regarding the HTTP status codes returned when GitHub API rate limits are exceeded (change 500 to 403/429).
Clarify or re-evaluate the claims that invalid JSON payloads or insufficient PAT scopes can directly result in a 500 error from the GitHub API; it is more common to receive a 400 or 403.
Provide more direct official GitHub documentation links for each specific claim, especially for the stated causes of 500 errors to enhance accuracy.
Add official or primary source URLs for unsupported claims.

sources.title: Common HTTP Errors and How to Debug Them - GraphQL
sources.claim: 500 Internal Server Error : Unexpected server failures. What it means. Something went wrong on the server.

sources.title: Best practices for using the REST API - GitHub Docs
sources.claim: The example error message format `{"message": "Internal Server Error", "documentation_url": "https://docs.github.com/rest"}` is consistent with GitHub's documentation style.

sources.title: Rate limits for the REST API - GitHub Docs
sources.claim: The `Server: GitHub.com` header is shown in API response examples.

sources.title: API Versions - GitHub Docs
sources.claim: The `X-GitHub-Api-Version` header should be used to specify an API version. Requests without this header will default to the `2022-11-28` version.

sources.title: Versioning Your REST API Made Easy: Tips and Tricks - High-Level Developer
sources.claim: The GitHub API supports header versioning, and clients can specify the desired API version using the `X-GitHub-Api-Version` header.

sources.title: Rate limits for the REST API - GitHub Docs
sources.claim: Authenticated users' requests count towards a personal rate limit of 5,000 requests per hour.

sources.title: Mastering GraphQL Error Handling: Strategies, Best Practices, and Future Trends - Testfully
sources.claim: Unlike traditional REST APIs, GraphQL always returns a 200 OK status code, even when an error occurs, with error details provided in the response body under the `errors` field.

sources.title: Error handling in GraphQL - Veritone
sources.claim: If a 500 error is returned for a GraphQL API request, it indicates a serious server-side failure that would not happen under normal circumstances.

sources.title: The Complete Guide to GitHub Personal Access Tokens (PATs) - Git-Zen
sources.claim: When configuring a Personal Access Token (PAT), it is necessary to review token permissions and set appropriate scopes, such as `repo`, `workflow`, `user`, or `gist`.

sources.title: Rate limits for the REST API - GitHub Docs
sources.claim: All responses include rate limit headers such as `X-RateLimit-Limit`, `X-RateLimit-Remaining`, and `X-RateLimit-Reset` to determine the current status of the primary rate limit.

sources.title: Best practices for using the REST API - GitHub Docs
sources.claim: The article's reference to `https://docs.github.com/en/rest/guides/best-practices-for-using-the-rest-api` is a valid link to official GitHub documentation for best practices.

sources.title: GitHub Support
sources.claim: The article correctly advises contacting GitHub Support at `https://support.github.com` for persistent 500 errors.

sources.title: GitHub - community/community: Public feedback discussions for: GitHub Mobile, GitHub Discussions, GitHub Codespaces, GitHub Sponsors, GitHub Issues and more!
sources.claim: The article's reference to `https://github.com/github-community/community/discussions` is a valid link to GitHub Community Discussions, which includes categories for API-related topics.

unsupported_claims: クライアント側の設定ミスではなく、サーバー側の障害またはAPIの不具合が原因です。(It is not due to client-side misconfiguration, but rather server-side failure or API malfunction.)

ただし、不正なリクエスト形式やタイムアウト、レート制限を超えた状態でも500が返されることがあり、実際には利用者側で対応可能な原因も含まれます。(However, a 500 can also be returned due to incorrect request format, timeouts, or exceeding rate limits, and thus includes causes that can be addressed by the user.)
原因1：不正なJSONペイロード形式またはエンコーディングエラー...500エラーで応答することがあります。(Cause 1: Invalid JSON payload format or encoding error...may respond with a 500 error.)
APIバージョン指定の不適切またはヘッダーの不足...予期しない形式のリクエストとして処理される結果500になります。(Improper API version specification or missing headers...processed as an unexpected request format, resulting in a 500.)

Personal Access Token（PAT）の権限不足：トークンに必要なスコープがない場合、実装によっては500で応答することがあります。(Insufficient Personal Access Token (PAT) permissions: If the token lacks the necessary scope, some implementations may respond with a 500.)
